companies.name: NVIDIA
companies.summary: The Russell 3000 reconstitution took effect after U.S. market close today, adding CoreWeave (CRWV) and IREN to the index — NVIDIA holds a ~$2 billion (~11%) stake in CoreWeave and a $2.1 billion investment option in IREN. Institutional passive-fund rebalancing begins Monday, June 29. NVIDIA's Annual Meeting proxy results (Form 8-K) are due by June 30, now 4 days away. NVDA closed at $195.74 on June 25 (-1.59%), within its 52-week range of $149.26–$236.54.

items.headline: Russell 3000 reconstitution effective today — CoreWeave and IREN join; institutional buying begins Monday June 29
items.detail: FTSE Russell's annual Russell 3000 reconstitution took effect after U.S. market close today, June 26. CoreWeave (CRWV) and IREN Limited join the index. NVIDIA holds approximately a $2 billion (~11%) stake in CoreWeave (purchased January 2026 at $87.20/share) and a $2.1 billion investment option in IREN. The dual inclusions trigger mandatory passive-fund rebalancing at Monday's open, June 29, increasing institutional visibility for NVIDIA's infrastructure investment network. CRWV closed at approximately $100.88 ahead of the inclusion.

items.headline: NVIDIA Annual Meeting proxy results due June 30 (4 days); BioNeMo Agent Toolkit launched at ISC Hamburg
items.detail: NVIDIA held its 2026 Annual Meeting of Stockholders on June 24. Proxy voting results will be filed via Form 8-K by June 30 — now 4 days away. Seven proposals were on the ballot including director elections, PwC ratification, executive compensation, and four shareholder proposals. At ISC High Performance 2026 in Hamburg, NVIDIA unveiled the BioNeMo Agent Toolkit for AI-driven life sciences pipelines and a warm-water cooling system that eliminates cooling-loop water use, with 35 AI HPC systems in development across European institutions.

items.headline: Vera Rubin in full production; DSX AI factory platform available for hyperscale deployment
items.detail: NVIDIA's Vera Rubin systems are ramping through a global manufacturing base for cloud providers and hyperscalers to deploy next-generation AI factories. The DSX platform — combining reference designs, simulation, and operating software — reduces token cost and speeds time to first production. NVIDIA and Microsoft expanded their Windows stack with RTX Spark and CUDA-native Windows agent runtimes for personal AI PCs.

companies.name: Alphabet / Google DeepMind
companies.category: Foundation models, search, cloud, and AI research
companies.summary: Gemini 3.5 Pro has been officially pushed to July 2026 — Google missed Sundar Pichai's self-imposed June 30 window. Noam Shazeer (VP Engineering, Gemini co-lead, 'Attention Is All You Need' co-author) departing for OpenAI is now confirmed, joining John Jumper's move to Anthropic as Google DeepMind's two biggest talent losses of 2026. Gemini 2.5 Pro with Deep Think (82.4% GPQA Diamond, beating Fable 5 at 79.1% and GPT-5.5 at 76.3%) remains the current flagship for AI Ultra subscribers ($250/month). GOOGL was near $343 as of June 25.

items.headline: Gemini 3.5 Pro officially delayed to July — Google misses June 30 self-imposed deadline
items.detail: Google DeepMind has pushed Gemini 3.5 Pro to July 2026, confirming CryptoBriefing's June 25 report. The model — expected to feature a 2M token context window, Deep Think reasoning, and pricing of ~$15 per million input tokens — remains in limited Vertex AI enterprise preview. Google cited quality refinements after early enterprise testing. The delay is Google's first publicly missed model window of 2026 and arrives simultaneously with its two most significant talent departures of the year.

items.headline: Noam Shazeer departure to OpenAI confirmed — fourth senior DeepMind researcher to leave in 2026
items.detail: Noam Shazeer — VP of Engineering, Gemini co-lead, and co-author of 'Attention Is All You Need' — has confirmed he is leaving Google for OpenAI. Google paid $2.7 billion in 2024 to license Character.AI technology and bring Shazeer back; his departure less than two years later is a significant blow. Combined with Nobel laureate John Jumper's June 19 departure to Anthropic, four senior Google DeepMind researchers have left for rival frontier labs in 2026. Both departures coincide with the Gemini 3.5 Pro delay.

items.headline: Gemini 2.5 Pro with Deep Think leads science-reasoning benchmarks at 82.4% GPQA Diamond
items.detail: Google DeepMind's Gemini 2.5 Pro with Deep Think mode — released June 22 for Google AI Ultra subscribers ($250/month) — posts 82.4% on GPQA Diamond science reasoning benchmarks, above Anthropic Fable 5 (79.1%) and OpenAI GPT-5.5 (76.3%). The model features a 2 million token context window. Deep Think mode runs at approximately 4x standard token cost for complex multi-step tasks. The Gemini 3.5 Pro delay means iOS 27's Siri AI will ship on earlier Gemini versions at general release.

companies.name: Microsoft
companies.category: Cloud AI, copilots, and enterprise software
companies.summary: Copilot Cowork usage-based billing grace period for Frontier preview tenants ends July 1 — now 5 days away. Organizations without billing configured lose Cowork access. Microsoft and Anthropic Maia 200 chip negotiations remain ongoing with no deal signed. Microsoft IQ is GA across GitHub Copilot, Foundry, and Copilot Studio. MSFT fell 3.45% to $352.83 on June 25.

items.headline: Copilot Cowork Frontier grace period ends July 1 — 5 days away; configure usage-based billing to retain access
items.detail: Microsoft Copilot Cowork reached general availability on June 16 under a Copilot Credits usage-based billing model ($0.01 per Credit, pay-as-you-go). Frontier preview tenants — any organization with at least one user who used Cowork during March 30–June 16 — have until July 1 before billing activates. Organizations without usage-based billing configured by July 1 will lose Copilot Cowork access. Microsoft 365 Business Standard and Business Premium with Copilot formalize as permanent commercial SKUs on the same date.

items.headline: Microsoft-Anthropic Maia 200 chip negotiations ongoing — no deal signed; first external customer would be landmark
items.detail: Microsoft and Anthropic remain in active negotiations (first reported May 21) to deploy Claude for inference on Maia 200 custom AI accelerators via Azure. Microsoft says Maia 200 can achieve more than 30% better performance-per-dollar than NVIDIA hardware for inference workloads. If finalized, Anthropic would be Maia 200's first external customer — extending Anthropic's multi-silicon strategy across SpaceX Colossus, AWS Trainium, CoreWeave, and Google/Broadcom TPU to also include Microsoft in-house silicon.

items.headline: Microsoft IQ GA; MAI-Thinking-1 and MAI-Image-2.5 available; exploring open-source for some Copilot Cowork workloads
items.detail: Microsoft IQ — Work IQ APIs plus Web IQ real-time grounding — is generally available across GitHub Copilot, Microsoft Foundry, and Copilot Studio. MAI-Thinking-1 (35B parameter reasoning model) and MAI-Image-2.5 are the newest in-house models from Build 2026 (June 2–3). Axios reported Microsoft is evaluating hosting a fine-tuned DeepSeek or comparable open-source model for some Copilot Cowork workloads to reduce API costs at agentic token scale — not a change for enterprise Foundry or flagship Copilot products.

companies.name: OpenAI
companies.summary: GPT-5.6 has entered a limited staged rollout coordinated with the White House OSTP — not broadly available, but selected partners now have access. OpenAI is leaning toward a 2027 IPO; CFO Sarah Friar cited Stargate cash burn and SpaceX IPO volatility. The 42-state AG probe (opened June 12) is ongoing. ChatGPT global market share fell below 50% for the first time (Sensor Tower: 46.4%, Gemini 27.7%, Claude 10.3%).

items.headline: GPT-5.6 enters limited staged rollout — coordinated with White House OSTP; broader availability expected July
items.detail: OpenAI has begun a limited, government-coordinated rollout of GPT-5.6 for selected partners, following coordination with the White House Office of the National Cyber Director and OSTP. The model is not yet broadly available; the consensus for general availability has shifted to July 2026. GPT-5.6 is expected to fix the 'Goblin Incident' reward model failure, include a redesigned reward audit pipeline, and achieve approximately 10–15% token efficiency gains over GPT-5.5. A 1.5 million token context window was observed in developer testing.

items.headline: OpenAI targets 2027 IPO — 42-state AG probe active; ChatGPT market share below 50% for first time
items.detail: OpenAI confidentially filed an S-1 with the SEC on June 8, led by Goldman Sachs, Morgan Stanley, and JPMorgan. The company is now leaning toward a 2027 listing; CFO Sarah Friar cited operational readiness concerns, Stargate cash burn, and SpaceX IPO volatility. A coalition of 42 state attorneys general (led by NY AG Letitia James) opened a formal probe on June 12 covering user harm, advertising practices, minors, and model sycophancy. Sensor Tower: ChatGPT fell to 46.4% global AI assistant market share — first time below 50% — with Gemini at 27.7% and Claude at 10.3%.

items.headline: Patch the Planet found 8 Linux kernel exploits and 5 Chrome V8 bugs; Appia Foundation co-founded for AI governance
items.detail: OpenAI's Patch the Planet initiative — launched June 22 in partnership with Trail of Bits and HackerOne — is automatically identifying, writing, testing, and merging security fixes for widely used open-source projects. Confirmed finds include 8 Linux kernel exploits, 5 Chrome V8 bugs, and vulnerabilities in cURL, Go, Python, and Sigstore. OpenAI also co-founded the Appia Foundation to build shared AI governance standards and a cross-industry trust layer.

companies.name: Anthropic
companies.summary: Commerce Secretary Lutnick's congressional deadline to provide written justification for the Fable 5/Mythos 5 export ban passed today, June 26, with no public response — Fable 5 remains offline on Day 14. Active deal talks between Anthropic and Trump administration officials are ongoing; Trump's June 20 Axios statement called Anthropic 'no longer a national security threat.' The biometric ID restoration pathway (Persona, effective July 8) is 12 days away. The AI for Science event with Nobel laureate John Jumper is June 30 (4 days). Anthropic raised a $65B Series H at $965B valuation (closed May 28). October 2026 Nasdaq IPO remains the target.

items.headline: Fable 5 ban Day 14 — Commerce Dept Lutnick deadline passed today with no public response; deal talks ongoing
items.detail: The bipartisan congressional deadline (set June 18) for Commerce Secretary Lutnick's written justification of the Fable 5/Mythos 5 export ban passed today, June 26, with no public response. Fable 5 and Mythos 5 remain globally suspended — zero active traffic confirmed. Anthropic has floated a proposal pledging closer White House cooperation and faster security-flaw remediation. Trump told Axios June 20 he no longer views Anthropic as a national security threat. The Globe and Mail confirmed active deal talks as of June 24. Polymarket assigns 57% odds of US restoration before July 1 and 75% by July 17.
items.sourceTitle: Fortune / explainx.ai / The Globe and Mail

items.headline: AI for Science event June 30 with John Jumper (4 days); biometric ID restoration pathway July 8 (12 days)
items.detail: Nobel laureate John Jumper — 2024 Nobel Prize winner in Chemistry and AlphaFold2 co-creator — joined Anthropic from Google DeepMind (announced June 19). His first public Anthropic appearance is confirmed for 'The Briefing: AI for Science' virtual event on June 30 at 10 AM PST — 4 days away — featuring life sciences executives and research demonstrations. Anthropic's July 8 privacy policy update enables optional Persona biometric ID verification, laying groundwork for a US-only Fable 5 restoration pathway for verified citizens — 12 days away.

items.headline: $65B Series H at $965B valuation closed May 28; October 2026 Nasdaq IPO target; revenue run rate exceeds $30B
items.detail: Anthropic closed a $65 billion Series H on May 28 at a $965 billion post-money valuation — the largest private AI fundraise ever — co-led by Altimeter, Dragoneer, Greenoaks, and Sequoia. The confidential S-1 filed June 1 targets an October 2026 Nasdaq IPO at a projected ~$1.1 trillion first-day market cap. Anthropic's revenue run rate exceeds $30 billion. The export ban on Fable 5 and Mythos 5 is the primary risk to the IPO timeline. Microsoft Maia 200 chip negotiations remain an additional potential compute channel.

companies.name: Meta
companies.category: Open models, consumer AI, and infrastructure
companies.summary: Meta Glasses launched June 23 at $299 (Fury and Adventurer) and $399 (Starfire) with Muse Spark AI — Meta's first closed-weight frontier model, scoring 52 on the Artificial Analysis Intelligence Index (fourth globally). Smart glasses shipments surged 167% YoY in Q1 2026; Meta holds 69.2% market share. Meta Llama 4 is the leading self-hostable frontier alternative amid the Fable 5 ban. META is down ~14% YTD. AMD MI450 samples shipping to Meta under the $60B/6GW partnership; CoreWeave $21B agreement through December 2032 proceeding.

items.headline: Meta Glasses launched June 23 at $299 with Muse Spark — Meta's first closed-weight frontier model
items.detail: Meta launched in-house Meta Glasses with EssilorLuxottica on June 23 at $299 (Fury and Adventurer) and $399 (Starfire; plus a Kylie Jenner edition) in 26 styles. All ship with Muse Spark, Meta's first closed-weight AI model from Meta Superintelligence Labs (Alexandr Wang's team), scoring 52 on the Artificial Analysis Intelligence Index — fourth globally. Live translation expanded to 20 languages including Mandarin, Korean, Japanese, Arabic, and Hindi. Smart glasses shipments surged 167% YoY in Q1 2026, with Meta at 69.2% market share.

items.headline: Meta Llama 4 emerges as leading enterprise self-hostable alternative during Fable 5 ban
items.detail: As Fable 5 and Mythos 5 remain suspended into Day 14, Meta Llama 4 has emerged as the leading self-hostable frontier-class model enterprises are evaluating as a hedge against government model access revocation risk. Open-weights posture is now a procurement variable following the June 12 suspension. CEO Arthur Mensch of Mistral AI has made parallel sovereignty arguments, and both reflect the industry's new awareness that centralized model access can be revoked by government directive.

items.headline: Meta AI Mode live on Facebook; 2026 capex $115–135B unchanged; AMD MI450 samples arriving
items.detail: Meta launched AI Mode on Facebook, using a three-tier routing system and social graph context for personalized recommendations inside Facebook search and feed. Meta's 2026 capital expenditure guidance of $115–135 billion is unchanged, supporting the AMD $60B/6GW partnership (MI450 samples now shipping), CoreWeave $21B agreement, and ongoing infrastructure buildout. New Meta Glasses add real-time AI inference workloads that will scale into both AMD and CoreWeave infrastructure.

companies.name: Amazon / AWS
companies.summary: Bedrock AgentCore is SOC 1/2/3 compliant with failure, intent, and trajectory insights GA. Grok 4.3 joined Bedrock's multi-model roster (AWS Weekly Roundup June 22). The Cerebras-AWS partnership adds CS-3 ultra-fast decode as a third silicon tier. Amazon invested $50B in Stargate (March 2026); Project Rainier Trainium2 cluster is running Anthropic workloads at scale. BMO Capital reiterated Buy with $355 target after AWS Summit.

items.headline: Bedrock AgentCore gains failure, intent, and trajectory insights — SOC 1/2/3 compliant; Web Search GA
items.detail: Amazon Bedrock AgentCore received an optimization update June 17, adding failure insights, intent insights, and trajectory insights that turn production agent traces into continuous improvement feedback loops. Web Search on AgentCore reached general availability. The service is now SOC 1, 2, and 3 compliant. AWS Summit New York (June 17–20) also launched AWS Continuum, AWS Context, and EC2 G7 instances with NVIDIA RTX PRO 4500 Blackwell Server Edition GPUs delivering 4.6x AI inference performance versus prior generation.

items.headline: Grok 4.3 now on Bedrock; Cerebras-AWS partnership adds third silicon tier for ultra-fast inference
items.detail: xAI's Grok 4.3 is now available on Amazon Bedrock with tool calling, structured output, and response streaming — adding xAI to Bedrock's multi-model roster alongside Claude, GPT-5.5, and Gemini. The Cerebras-AWS partnership (announced June 23) pairs Cerebras CS-3 chips for ultra-fast decode with AWS Trainium 3 for prefill as an inference disaggregation architecture, adding a third silicon tier alongside NVIDIA GPUs and Trainium for high-speed token generation.

items.headline: Amazon $50B Stargate investment confirmed; Project Rainier running Anthropic workloads at scale toward 1M+ Trainium2
items.detail: Amazon's $50 billion investment in the March 2026 Stargate funding round positions AWS as a core Stargate infrastructure partner. AWS Project Rainier — nearly half a million Trainium2 chips — is running Anthropic Claude workloads at scale, with Anthropic expecting more than 1 million Trainium2 chips by year end. Amazon's $100B+ over 10 years agreement with Anthropic secures up to 5GW of Trainium capacity across Trainium2, Trainium3, and Trainium4.

companies.name: xAI
companies.summary: SPCX is trading near $153–155 as of June 26, approximately 32% below its $225.64 all-time high reached June 16, but recovering from the $147.11 low on June 23. Both Colossus 1 and Colossus 2 are rented to third-party AI companies; Reflection AI's $150M/month Colossus 2 deal starts July 1 (5 days away). SpaceX's $60B all-stock Cursor (Anysphere) acquisition is progressing toward Q3 2026 close. Grok 4.3 is available on Amazon Bedrock.

items.headline: SPCX at ~$153–155 — recovering from $147.11 low; 32% below $225.64 ATH set June 16
items.detail: SpaceX (SPCX) is trading near $153–155 on June 26, recovering from the $147.11 all-time low set June 23, following the post-Cursor announcement correction from the $225.64 ATH on June 16. SpaceX priced its IPO at $135/share on June 11, raising $75 billion at a $1.75 trillion valuation — the largest IPO ever recorded. Both Colossus 1 (rented due to mixed GPU composition) and Colossus 2 (rented to Reflection AI starting July 1) are fully committed to third-party customers, raising ongoing questions about dedicated compute for Grok AI's own workloads.

items.headline: Reflection AI $150M/month Colossus 2 deal starts July 1 — 5 days away; Grok 4.3 on Bedrock with 1M token context
items.detail: SpaceX's $6.3 billion compute agreement with Reflection AI (valued at $25 billion, working with DoE Genesis Mission and Pentagon AI) begins July 1 — 5 days away. Reflection pays $150 million per month for NVIDIA GB300 GPU access at Colossus 2 in Memphis through 2029, with a 90-day termination clause after the first 3 months. Grok 4.3 — featuring a 1 million token context window and the lowest hallucination rate among frontier models — is now available on Amazon Bedrock with tool calling, structured output, and response streaming.

items.headline: $60B Cursor acquisition progressing toward Q3 close; SpaceX and Cursor jointly training new model for Grok Build
items.detail: SpaceX's $60 billion all-stock acquisition of Anysphere (Cursor AI) — confirmed via SEC Form 8-K, with Cursor having over $2 billion ARR and more than half the Fortune 500 as clients — is progressing toward Q3 2026 regulatory close. SpaceX and Cursor are jointly training a new model for Grok Build, competing directly with Anthropic Claude Code, GitHub Copilot, and Google coding agents. Grok Build now includes /goal autonomous mode, clean diffs, and parallel subagents.

companies.name: Broadcom
companies.category: AI networking, custom silicon, and infrastructure software
companies.summary: AVGO is trading near $378–380. The $0.65 per share quarterly cash dividend is payable June 30 — 4 days away — marking 14 consecutive years of dividend increases. The Apollo and Blackstone-led $35B AI XPV Platform is operational, enabling 20GW+ of Broadcom XPU compute for hyperscalers including Anthropic and OpenAI through 2028. Broadcom is fabricating OpenAI's first custom 'Titan' AI chip on TSMC 3nm for H2 2026 Stargate deployment. Q2 AI semiconductor revenue was $10.8B (+143% YoY); Q3 guidance is approximately $16B.

items.headline: Broadcom quarterly dividend $0.65/share payable June 30 (4 days); Q2 AI revenue $10.8B +143% YoY; Q3 guidance ~$16B
items.detail: Broadcom's $0.65 per share quarterly cash dividend — marking 14 consecutive years of increases — is payable June 30 to holders of record June 22. Q2 FY2026 AI semiconductor revenue was $10.8 billion (+143% YoY) on record quarterly revenue and free cash flow. Q3 AI semiconductor revenue guidance is approximately $16 billion. FY2027 target exceeds $100 billion across six core custom-silicon customers. Tomahawk 6 (Davisson) — the industry's first 102.4 Tbps Ethernet switch with co-packaged optics — launched mid-June.

items.headline: Apollo and Blackstone $35B AI XPV Platform operational — enables Anthropic 1GW+ XPU compute at Fluidstack sites
items.detail: The Apollo Global Management and Blackstone-led $35 billion capital solution for the Broadcom AI XPV Platform — described as the largest private financing ever executed — is operational and enables more than 20 gigawatts of global AI compute using Broadcom XPUs for hyperscalers including Anthropic and OpenAI through 2028. The first tranche facilitates Anthropic's 1+ gigawatt compute expansion at Fluidstack-based sites beginning mid-2026. Apollo president Jim Zelter appeared on CNBC June 22 to detail the deal.

items.headline: Broadcom fabricating OpenAI Titan chip on TSMC 3nm for H2 2026 Stargate deployment
items.detail: Broadcom is fabricating OpenAI's first custom 'Titan' AI accelerator on TSMC's 3nm process, with mass production targeted for H2 2026 for Stargate data center deployment. Titan is designed to complement NVIDIA GPUs for large-scale inference workloads. Broadcom's position as the leading custom ASIC partner for hyperscale AI silicon programs is reinforced by its six confirmed custom-silicon customers including Anthropic, Google, Meta, and OpenAI.

companies.name: Palantir
companies.summary: PLTR hit a fresh 52-week low of $107.27 on June 25 (-5.50%), down ~40% year-to-date despite Q1 2026 blowout results ($1.6B revenue +85% YoY, US commercial +133%) and strong contract wins. Palantir won a U.S. Treasury Department contract June 23. The Anthropic-Palantir-AWS defense pipeline remains affected by the Fable/Mythos ban; the Pentagon reportedly migrated two-thirds of AI workflows off Anthropic. The Commerce Dept deadline passing today with no response leaves the defense pipeline status unchanged.

items.headline: PLTR at 52-week low $107.27 — down 5.50% on June 25, off ~40% YTD despite blowout Q1 and contract wins
items.detail: Palantir closed at $107.27 on June 25, a fresh 52-week low, after falling 5.50% in the session. The stock is down approximately 40% year-to-date despite posting blowout Q1 2026 results ($1.6B revenue, +85% YoY; US commercial +133% — fastest growth in company history) and winning multiple large government contracts. High-profile short sellers, valuation concerns above 120x trailing P/E, and intensifying competition from frontier AI labs are the primary headwinds. AIP is now available on Google Cloud Marketplace with BigQuery and Gemini integrations.

items.headline: Palantir won U.S. Treasury contract June 23; Army $10B and DHS $1B deals extend federal dominance
items.detail: The U.S. Treasury Department awarded Palantir a contract on June 23 for a common API layer supporting developer platforms, workflow automation, and data analytics for IT modernization. 2026 federal wins: $10 billion 10-year U.S. Army enterprise AI services; $1 billion 5-year DHS AI platform; $300 million USDA deal (April). Maven Smart System retains its DoD program-of-record designation with usage quadrupled over 12 months. 2026 revenue guidance: $7.18–$7.20 billion (+61% YoY).

items.headline: Anthropic-Palantir-AWS defense pipeline unchanged — Commerce Dept deadline passed today with no response
items.detail: The Commerce Dept Lutnick deadline passed today, June 26, with no public response, leaving the Fable 5/Mythos 5 export ban and its effect on the Anthropic-Palantir-AWS defense pipeline unchanged. The Pentagon reportedly migrated at least two-thirds of AI workflows off Anthropic's models. Maven Smart System retains its DoD program-of-record designation; Claude remains integrated for eligible non-Fable workflows. Active deal talks between Anthropic and Trump administration officials (confirmed by The Globe and Mail) continue.

companies.name: Apple
companies.summary: iOS 27 second developer beta (released June 22) adds RCS inline replies and 'Write with Siri.' The rebuilt Siri powered by a custom Google Gemini model (~$1B/year license) targets GA September 2026. iOS 27's AI Extensions framework makes Claude, ChatGPT, Gemini, and Mistral eligible as Siri extensions — disabled pending EU DMA clarity. OpenAI retained outside counsel and is weighing a breach-of-contract notice over ChatGPT placement; no lawsuit filed. AAPL fell 1.42% to $291.44.

items.headline: iOS 27 second developer beta released June 22 — Siri AI runs on Gemini; AI Extensions support Claude, Mistral, ChatGPT
items.detail: iOS 27's second developer beta (June 22) adds RCS inline replies and 'Write with Siri.' The rebuilt Siri AI runs on a custom ~1.2 trillion parameter Google Gemini model via a three-tier routing system: on-device for fast queries, Private Cloud Compute for medium complexity, and Gemini for complex multi-step tasks. The iOS 27 Extensions framework lets users choose ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini, or Mistral as their default Siri AI extension — currently disabled pending EU Digital Markets Act regulatory clarity. General release targets September 2026.

items.headline: OpenAI retained outside counsel over Siri placement; weighing breach-of-contract notice; no lawsuit filed
items.detail: OpenAI retained outside counsel and is weighing a formal breach-of-contract notice against Apple over the perceived lack of prime ChatGPT placement in the new Siri AI. OpenAI alleges Apple made 'no honest effort' to meet integration commitments; iPhone users far prefer the standalone ChatGPT app over the Siri integration. AAPL fell 1.42% to $291.44 on this news. No lawsuit has been filed as of June 26. Apple is separately evaluating Mistral AI as an additional provider option.

companies.name: AMD
companies.category: AI accelerators, CPUs, and data center silicon
companies.summary: AMD has recovered to approximately $532–544 after the 6.3% decline on June 23 caused by SK Hynix slowing HBM4 expansion. Analysts confirmed the selloff was a supply-chain margin concern — AMD's H2 2026 MI450 production timeline is on track. MI450 samples continue shipping to OpenAI (6GW deal, 1GW starting H2 2026) and Meta ($60B/6GW). AMD Advancing AI 2026 is July 22–23 at Moscone Center. Q2 2026 results report August 4.

items.headline: AMD recovering at ~$532–544 — analysts confirm H2 2026 MI450 timeline intact despite SK Hynix HBM4 news
items.detail: AMD shares recovered after the 6.3% decline on June 23 triggered by reports that SK Hynix is slowing HBM4 memory expansion to redirect capacity to conventional DRAM. Analysts at Goldman Sachs (Buy, $450 target), Baird ($625), and Barclays (Street-high $665) characterized the selloff as a supply-chain margin concern, not a demand signal for AMD's MI450 GPU roadmap. AMD CEO confirmed MI450 samples are shipping to both OpenAI and Meta; Helios rack systems remain on target for H2 2026 production.

items.headline: MI450 samples shipping to OpenAI and Meta; AMD Advancing AI 2026 is July 22–23; Q2 results August 4
items.detail: AMD CEO Lisa Su confirmed MI450 GPU samples are shipping to lead customers OpenAI (6GW partnership, beginning with 1GW MI450 in H2 2026) and Meta ($60B/5-year, 6GW, custom MI450). AMD denied MI455X delay reports; Helios rack systems remain on H2 2026 production target. AMD Advancing AI 2026 is July 22–23 at Moscone Center in San Francisco — expected to cover the formal MI450 launch, EPYC Venice Zen 6 CPUs, Helios rack confirmation, and MI500 series preview. Q2 2026 financial results: August 4.

companies.name: Perplexity AI
companies.summary: Comet AI browser is globally available on Mac and iOS, now running on Claude Opus 4.6 by default, and reached #3 in the App Store within 48 hours of iOS launch. Comet Enterprise is available via MDM deployment on macOS/Windows. Perplexity Computer runs as native side panels in Microsoft 365 apps. CEO Aravind Srinivas confirmed 2028 IPO target. Apple-Perplexity acquisition talks are dead. ARR reached $500M (April 2026, +335% YoY); 100M+ MAU; $20B valuation; $750M Azure infrastructure deal.

items.headline: Comet browser globally available on Mac and iOS — Claude Opus 4.6 default; MDM deployment for enterprise
items.detail: Perplexity's Comet AI browser is globally available on Mac and iOS, running on Claude Opus 4.6 by default (users may also select other models). Enterprise admins can deploy Comet via MDM on macOS and Windows. The iOS version reached #3 in the App Store within 48 hours of launch. iOS updates added phone number actions, iPad polish, and 8 additional improvements. Comet Enterprise launched March 17 across two commercial tiers.

items.headline: Apple acquisition talks dead; CEO confirms 2028 IPO; Perplexity Computer live in Microsoft 365; $500M ARR
items.detail: Apple-Perplexity acquisition discussions are confirmed dead — Perplexity management was never formally approached and no offer was made. CEO Aravind Srinivas publicly stated a preference for an independent 2028 IPO (June 9 CNBC), explicitly not accelerating despite Anthropic and OpenAI moving toward public markets. Perplexity Computer is live as native side panels in Word, Excel, PowerPoint, Outlook, and Teams. ARR reached $500M (April 2026, +335% YoY) with 100M+ monthly active users. May 2026 funding raised $200M at $20B valuation; total raised ~$1.72B. $750M Azure infrastructure deal in place.

companies.name: CoreWeave
companies.summary: CRWV's Russell 3000 inclusion took effect after market close today, June 26 — institutional passive-fund rebalancing begins at Monday's open, June 29. CRWV traded at approximately $100.88 ahead of the inclusion. CoreWeave joined the Nasdaq-100 on June 22 (closed at $117.03, +9.67%). Nine of the top ten AI model providers are customers. Contracted backlog is approaching ~$131 billion; 2026 capex floor is $31 billion for Vera Rubin NVL72 buildout. A $3.1B HPC loan was recently secured.

items.headline: Russell 3000 effective today June 26 — institutional rebalancing begins Monday June 29; CRWV at ~$100.88
items.detail: CoreWeave (CRWV) entered the Russell 3000 index effective after market close today, June 26. Institutional passive-fund rebalancing buying begins at Monday's open, June 29. CRWV traded at approximately $100.88 ahead of the inclusion — just above $100 — after joining the Nasdaq-100 on June 22 (closed at $117.03, +9.67%) and then pulling back on profit-taking. NVIDIA holds approximately a $2 billion (~11%) stake in CRWV (purchased at $87.20/share in January 2026). IREN Limited also joins the Russell 3000 today.

items.headline: Backlog approaching $131B; 2026 capex floor $31B for Vera Rubin NVL72; $3.1B HPC loan secured
items.detail: CoreWeave's contracted backlog is approaching approximately $131 billion — up roughly $32 billion since Q1 2026 reporting — driven by Vera Rubin NVL72 buildout for Meta, Anthropic, and OpenAI. The 2026 capex floor was raised to $31 billion. A $3.1 billion HPC loan was recently secured to support the buildout. Cerebras Q1 2026 revenue of $193.4 million (3x analyst estimates, +92–94% YoY) validates the AI inference demand powering CoreWeave's backlog growth. The Cerebras-OpenAI $20B+ compute deal confirms compute diversification while CoreWeave maintains anchor contracts.

companies.name: Mistral AI
companies.summary: Mistral's Bruyères-le-Châtel data center near Paris (44 MW, 13,800 NVIDIA GB300 GPU systems, operated by Eclairion) is still within its end-of-June opening window — 4 days remaining as of June 26 — with no official announcement yet. NVIDIA and Mistral launched Mistral Compute sovereign AI GPU cloud at VivaTech on June 20. Mistral secured an $830M debt facility from a seven-bank consortium. iOS 27's AI Extensions framework makes Mistral eligible for Siri integration. CEO Mensch is positioning Mistral as the open-source sovereignty alternative amid the Fable ban.

items.headline: Paris data center end-of-June opening window: 4 days remaining — no official announcement as of June 26
items.detail: Mistral AI's Bruyères-le-Châtel data center near Paris (44 MW, 13,800 NVIDIA GB300 GPU systems providing approximately 276 exaflops of FP4 compute, operated by Eclairion) is expected to open by end of June 2026 — 4 days remaining. No official opening announcement has been made as of June 26. A secondary Les Ulis 10 MW inference facility is scheduled for Q3 2026. The Bruyères facility is the first node in Mistral's planned 200 MW European compute roadmap and the flagship of Mistral Compute.

items.headline: NVIDIA-Mistral Compute launched at VivaTech; $830M debt facility; Apple iOS 27 Extensions makes Mistral Siri-eligible
items.detail: NVIDIA and Mistral AI launched Mistral Compute — a sovereign AI GPU cloud platform for European customers and governments — at VivaTech 2026 (June 20), built on 18,000 NVIDIA Grace Blackwell systems. Mistral is a founding member of the NVIDIA Nemotron Coalition. An $830 million debt facility from seven French and international banks funds the data center buildout. Apple's iOS 27 AI Extensions framework — disabled pending EU DMA clarity — lists Mistral as an eligible Siri AI option alongside Claude, ChatGPT, and Gemini.

items.headline: CEO Mensch leverages Fable ban for sovereignty narrative; targets EUR 1B 2026 revenue
items.detail: CEO Arthur Mensch is actively leveraging European concern over the Fable/Mythos export suspension — now Day 14 — to position Mistral as the open-source AI that neither a government nor corporation can revoke. Mensch stated: 'We exist to make sure that everyone gets access to the best AI systems, outside of centralised control.' Mistral targets EUR 1 billion in 2026 revenue with 1,000 employees. The Vibe unified AI agent (Work Mode, Code Mode, VS Code extension) launched at VivaTech June 20.

companies.name: Tesla
companies.category: Autonomous vehicles, robotics, and applied AI
companies.summary: TSLA is trading near flat at ~$375. Cybercab production continues at Giga Texas with 102+ units in the outbound lot; the unsupervised Robotaxi Service covers the full Austin metro, Dallas, Houston, and the SF Bay Area. Optimus Gen 3 is in mass production at Fremont with V3 full-body production targeting summer 2026. Tesla's Terafab in-house semiconductor fab for the AI5 chip was announced March 21. Elon Musk told investors June 20 to be 'really patient' with both Optimus and the Cybercab ramp.

items.headline: Cybercab at 102+ units in outbound lot; full Austin metro covered; robotaxi active in Dallas, Houston, SF Bay Area
items.detail: Tesla's Cybercab has 102+ units in the outbound lot at Giga Texas. The unsupervised Robotaxi Service covers the entire Austin metro (expanded June 8) and is active in Dallas, Houston, and the SF Bay Area. EPA filing confirmed full specs: 47.6 kWh battery, 163 kW motor, 418-mile unadjusted range (293-mile adjusted), 3,113 lb curb weight. Elon Musk told investors June 20 to be 'really patient' with the Cybercab ramp. The service has faced early criticism for long waits and stalled rides.

items.headline: Optimus Gen 3 in mass production; Terafab in-house AI5 chip fab; 10M robot/year facility at Giga Texas North
items.detail: Tesla's Optimus Gen 3 humanoid robot entered mass production at Fremont in January 2026, with V3 full-body production targeting summer 2026. Tesla announced the Terafab initiative March 21 — an in-house semiconductor fab for the AI5 chip powering Optimus Gen 3. The Gigafactory Texas North Campus expansion (5.2 million square feet) targets 10 million robots per year. Approximately 300–500 total Optimus units exist, all in learning/data-collection phase. FSD v14.3 launched April with 20% reduced inference latency.

ipoTracker.status: Fable/Mythos ban Day 14; Commerce Dept Lutnick deadline passed today June 26 with no public response; biometric ID pathway July 8 (12 days); AI for Science event June 30 (4 days); $65B Series H at $965B valuation closed May 28; October 2026 Nasdaq IPO target
ipoTracker.process: Anthropic confirmed its confidential draft Form S-1 to the SEC on June 1, 2026. The $65B Series H closed May 28 at a $965 billion post-money valuation — the largest private AI fundraise ever, co-led by Altimeter, Dragoneer, Greenoaks, and Sequoia. Target listing: October 2026 at a projected ~$1.1 trillion first-day market cap. Revenue run rate exceeds $30 billion. As of June 26 (Day 14), Fable 5 and Mythos 5 remain globally suspended — zero active traffic. Commerce Secretary Lutnick's congressional deadline for written justification passed today with no public response. A potential US-only restoration mechanism is tied to Persona biometric ID verification (effective July 8 — 12 days away). Nobel laureate John Jumper has joined Anthropic; first public appearance is June 30 AI for Science event (4 days). Microsoft Maia 200 chip negotiations ongoing.
ipoTracker.note: Commerce Dept deadline passed today with no response — Day 14 ban continues. July 8 biometric ID pathway (12 days) and June 30 AI for Science event (4 days) are the next public milestones. August 1 is the 60-day executive order deadline for the NSA/Treasury/CISA AI risk framework.

latestArticle.title: Anthropic files to go public

ipoTracker.status: S-1 filed June 8; GPT-5.6 in limited staged rollout (government-coordinated); 2027 IPO leaning confirmed; 42-state AG probe ongoing; ChatGPT market share below 50% for first time
ipoTracker.process: OpenAI confirmed confidential S-1 filing with the SEC on June 8, 2026. Goldman Sachs, Morgan Stanley, and JPMorgan are leading. The company is leaning toward a 2027 listing; CFO Sarah Friar cited operational readiness concerns, Stargate cash burn, and SpaceX IPO volatility. GPT-5.6 has entered a limited, government-coordinated staged rollout (coordinated with White House OSTP) — not yet broadly available; broader availability expected July 2026. A 42-state attorney general coalition opened a formal probe June 12. Sensor Tower: ChatGPT fell to 46.4% global AI assistant market share (first time below 50%), with Gemini at 27.7% and Claude at 10.3%.
ipoTracker.note: GPT-5.6 limited rollout is a new development as of June 25–26. 2027 IPO preference confirmed. 42-state AG probe and Florida civil suit are pre-IPO risk factors. EU AI Act chatbot enforcement begins August 2, 2026.

latestArticle.title: OpenAI staggers GPT-5.6 rollout, eyes 2027 IPO

ipoTracker.status: SPCX ~$153–155 on June 26 (-32% from ATH of $225.64 on June 16; recovering from $147.11 low June 23); Reflection AI Colossus 2 deal starts July 1 (5 days); Cursor $60B acquisition pending Q3 close
ipoTracker.process: SpaceX priced at $135/share on June 11, raising $75 billion at a $1.75 trillion valuation — the largest IPO ever recorded. Shares peaked at $225.64 on June 16 following the Cursor announcement, then fell to a $147.11 low on June 23. Both Colossus 1 (mixed GPU composition) and Colossus 2 (committed to Reflection AI starting July 1) are rented to third-party AI companies — Anthropic ($1.25B/month through May 2029), Google (~$920M/month), and Reflection AI ($150M/month from July 1). SpaceX confirmed the $60B all-stock acquisition of Anysphere (Cursor AI) via SEC Form 8-K; Q3 2026 regulatory close expected. xAI was folded into SpaceX as the SpaceXAI division in February 2026.
ipoTracker.confidence: Confirmed; public company as of June 12, 2026
ipoTracker.note: Reflection AI $150M/month Colossus 2 deal starts July 1 — 5 days away. SPCX recovering from $147.11 low. Cursor Q3 close is the next major corporate milestone.

ipoTracker.status: CBRS at ~$181.59 on June 26 (recovering from $168.52 post-earnings selloff June 25); Q1 2026 massively beat estimates ($193.4M vs $56.65M); $20B+ OpenAI deal; AWS partnership; margin compression guidance drove selloff

Two major deals announced alongside earnings: a multi-year OpenAI compute deal (750MW, $20B+ valued, option to $30B) and a multi-year AWS partnership pairing Cerebras CS-3 with AWS Trainium 3. CBRS is recovering toward ~$181.59 on June 26, up from the $168.52 close on June 25 (-7.61%) as forward gross margin compression guidance triggered selling. FY2026 core revenue guidance: $855.5M–$865M (69% growth at midpoint).
ipoTracker.note: CBRS recovering from post-earnings margin guidance selloff. The $20B+ OpenAI deal places Cerebras on par with CoreWeave's Meta deal ($21B). Q1 revenue 3x analyst estimates — one of the biggest AI infrastructure earnings surprises of 2026.

The IPO was the first major public listing of a company whose primary assets are open-source model repositories, training infrastructure, and developer community. Hugging Face operates the largest public repository of open-source AI models and datasets. The stock was trading around $46 per share in mid-June.

AgentCore GA provides the enterprise deployment layer with failure, intent, and trajectory insights. The Cerebras-AWS partnership adds CS-3 as a complementary silicon tier for ultra-fast decode. Claude models (outside Fable 5 and Mythos 5) remain available to AWS customers.

partnershipTracker.type: AI compute and cloud GPU partnership
partnershipTracker.status: Active strategic relationship; MI-series GPUs on Azure; AMD recovering from June 23 SK Hynix selloff; AMD Advancing AI July 22–23
partnershipTracker.terms: AMD MI300X GPU instances are deployed on Azure for AI inference workloads alongside NVIDIA hardware. Microsoft IQ went GA June 16, driving more enterprise agent workloads onto Azure infrastructure where AMD silicon competes for inference capacity. Microsoft is exploring open-source model alternatives for some Copilot Cowork workloads — not an AMD hardware change. AMD's Advancing AI 2026 event (July 22–23) may include additional Azure-related disclosures.
partnershipTracker.confidence: Ongoing relationship; no new announced terms
partnershipTracker.note: AMD Advancing AI 2026 on July 22–23 is the next potential milestone for Azure-related disclosures. AMD stock recovering from June 23 SK Hynix selloff.

latestArticle.title: AMD at Microsoft Build 2026

partnershipTracker.status: Confirmed $60B, ~6GW multi-year agreement; MI450 samples shipping to Meta; Meta Glasses launch adds new AI inference demand
partnershipTracker.terms: AMD and Meta's 6-gigawatt, roughly $60 billion strategic partnership covers multiple generations of AMD Instinct GPUs co-developed via the Helios architecture through Open Compute Project, with shipments beginning H2 2026. AMD CEO confirmed MI450 samples are shipping to Meta. The agreement includes a 160-million-share AMD warrant giving Meta a potential 10% stake. Meta Glasses launch (June 23) adds real-time AI inference workloads that will scale into AMD-powered infrastructure.
partnershipTracker.confidence: Confirmed by both companies; samples shipping per AMD CEO
partnershipTracker.note: Meta Glasses add AI glasses inference demand. AMD Advancing AI 2026 on July 22–23 may include additional Meta deployment disclosures. SK Hynix selloff does not affect the Meta-AMD deployment timeline.

latestArticle.title: AMD and Meta Announce Expanded Strategic Partnership to Deploy 6 Gigawatts of AMD GPUs

partnershipTracker.type: Infrastructure equity investment and multi-generational GPU supply
partnershipTracker.status: Confirmed deep strategic partnership; Russell 3000 effective today June 26 for both CRWV and IREN; institutional rebalancing begins Monday June 29
partnershipTracker.terms: NVIDIA invested roughly $2 billion in CoreWeave Class A common stock (~11% stake, purchased January 2026 at $87.20/share) and the companies committed to accelerating more than 5GW of AI factories by 2030. CoreWeave completed the industry-first Vera Rubin NVL72 bring-up on June 1. AI compute backlog approaching ~$131 billion. CoreWeave joined Nasdaq-100 on June 22; Russell 3000 effective today June 26. IREN — NVIDIA holds a $2.1B investment option — also joins the Russell 3000 today.
partnershipTracker.confidence: Confirmed by both companies
partnershipTracker.note: Russell 3000 effective today June 26 for both CRWV and IREN — institutional rebalancing begins Monday June 29. At ~$100.88, NVIDIA's CRWV stake is up ~15.7% from the $87.20 purchase price.

latestArticle.title: CoreWeave (CRWV) and IREN join Russell 3000 Index

partnershipTracker.status: Confirmed partnership; Mistral Compute launched VivaTech June 20; Paris data center end-of-June window with 4 days remaining; iOS 27 AI Extensions makes Mistral Siri-eligible
partnershipTracker.terms: NVIDIA and Mistral AI launched Mistral Compute — a sovereign AI GPU cloud platform for European customers and governments — at VivaTech 2026 (June 20). Mistral is a founding member of the NVIDIA Nemotron Coalition for open frontier models. Mistral Compute is built on 18,000 NVIDIA Grace Blackwell systems. Mistral's Bruyères-le-Châtel data center near Paris (44 MW, 13,800 NVIDIA GB300 systems, operated by Eclairion) is expected to open by end of June — 4 days remaining; no official opening announcement yet.
partnershipTracker.confidence: Confirmed by both companies at VivaTech
partnershipTracker.note: Paris data center end-of-June opening window has 4 days remaining. iOS 27 AI Extensions framework makes Mistral a potential Siri integration candidate — a new enterprise distribution path.

latestArticle.title: France Advances Europe's AI Future With NVIDIA Technologies

partnershipTracker.type: AI compute contract — Colossus 2 data center access
partnershipTracker.status: Confirmed $6.3B deal — $150M/month starts July 1 (5 days); Colossus 2 dedicated to Reflection AI
partnershipTracker.terms: SpaceX signed a $6.3 billion compute agreement with open-source AI startup Reflection AI (last valued at $25 billion, working with DoE Genesis Mission and Pentagon AI), providing access to NVIDIA GB300 GPUs via the Colossus 2 data center in Memphis. Reflection pays $150 million per month starting July 1 — 5 days away — through 2029, with a 90-day termination clause after the first 3-month period. This confirms Colossus operates as a multi-tenant commercial AI data center platform.
partnershipTracker.confidence: Confirmed by CNBC reporting
partnershipTracker.note: July 1 start date is 5 days away. Combined with Colossus 1 being rented out, SpaceX operates Colossus as a commercial multi-tenant data center — raising ongoing questions about Grok AI's own dedicated compute access.

latestArticle.title: SpaceX AI Colossus data center signs deal with Reflection AI

partnershipTracker.type: Structured finance platform for Broadcom AI XPU deployment
partnershipTracker.status: Operational — $35B capital solution; enables 20GW+ Broadcom XPU compute for Anthropic and OpenAI through 2028; Broadcom dividend payable June 30 (4 days)
partnershipTracker.terms: Apollo Global Management and Blackstone are leading a $35 billion capital solution for the Broadcom AI XPV Platform, enabling more than 20 gigawatts of global AI compute capacity using Broadcom XPUs for hyperscalers including Anthropic and OpenAI through 2028. Platform operational since June 9. Anthropic's 1+ gigawatt compute expansion at Fluidstack-based sites beginning mid-2026 is among the first deployments. Broadcom $0.65 quarterly dividend payable June 30 — 4 days away.
partnershipTracker.confidence: Confirmed by Apollo press release and Broadcom announcement
partnershipTracker.note: Platform operational. Broadcom $0.65 quarterly dividend payable June 30 (4 days). Q3 AI semiconductor revenue guidance ~$16B.

latestArticle.title: Broadcom, Apollo, and Blackstone Establish Landmark Strategic Platform to Accelerate 20GW+ of Global AI Deployments
